name: Data Privacy Act (RA 10173)
summary: Governs processing of personal data. Requires 72-hour breach notification, data protection officers, privacy impact assessments, and consent management.
requirements: 72-hour breach notification to NPC and affected data subjects
Privacy Impact Assessments (PIA) for new systems and processes
Data subject rights: access, correction, erasure, portability
Consent management with clear purpose limitation
Data sharing agreements with proper legal basis documentation
penalties: Criminal penalties of 1-6 years imprisonment and fines of ₱500K-₱5M per violation. NPC can issue compliance orders, temporary bans on data processing, and recommend prosecution.

sasCapabilityMapping.specificFeature: Data lineage tracking for rapid breach impact assessment
Data cataloging and classification for PIA automation
Data discovery and subject access request workflows
Consent-aware decisioning with purpose limitation enforcement

competitivePositioning: SAS Data Management provides the data governance foundation (lineage, cataloging, quality, masking) that underpins DPA compliance. While competitors focus on analytics, SAS provides end-to-end data lifecycle governance required by the NPC.
talkingPoints: NPC enforcement is increasing — 2024 saw record compliance orders
72-hour breach notification requires knowing exactly what data was affected — SAS data lineage enables this
Privacy Impact Assessments need data cataloging — SAS automates discovery and classification
Data masking/anonymization built into SAS Data Management for development and testing environments
SAS supports consent-aware analytics — process only data with proper legal basis
